The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Downtown Titans
Date: March 23-30


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Downtown Titans—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Volcano Circuit CCW
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

description: Come and join TNP as we smash a crit race out, can you stick with the big hitters and claim the win!

name: Chill 'n' Spin Sunday Ride
description:
It’s time to join Team CLS for our Sunday Chill ‘n’ Spin ride… and UNLOCK our awesome Team CLS Kit.

Spin the legs and enjoy some banter. While stronger riders may choose to use this event as a recovery ride, this will also be an opportunity to get in a solid ride at mid D cat pace.

The focus is on riding with the bunch around the beacon as closely as possible. We see this ride as being a great event for riders that are:
- New to zwift, or have not tried a group ride before
- Recovering from injury
- Looking for a controlled group ride at D cat pace
- Easing back on the intensity and want a ride with plenty of chat.

Like all our events please make sure you can hold the above pace as we cannot reduce this at the expense of other riders although we will help to tow you back for dropouts etc.

WARNING - Please only attempt sneaky sprints on this ride if you feel your legs are sufficiently warmed up to do so. Sudden high intensity efforts on cold muscles is not advised. 


WEEKLY PROGRAM
Mon  18:50 UK - Team CLS Racing - Pace ‘n’ Race: Recon and Activation 
Tues 18:50 UK - This ‘n’ That - Endurance Training
Wed  18:50 UK - Ride ‘n’ Explore Club Rideout
Thur 18:50 UK - High ‘n’ Low - Interval Training
Sat  08:50 and 14:45 UK - Base ‘n’ Banter Endurance ride
Sun  18:50 UK - Chill 'n' Spin - Weekend Social


TEAM CLS LINKS
Check out www.teamclscc.co.uk ...find out about our club, current events and leaders. 

Facebook Club Site - https://www.facebook.com/groups/teamclscc
Facebook Racing Site - https://www.facebook.com/groups/teamclsracing
Zwift Power Team - https://www.zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=1426



TEAM | CLS - Training | Racing | Community

name: Wakey-Wakey Kiwi Crew
description:
Join Johan Oelofse and the awesome Crew on the ride created by the legendary Greg "Coach Hendy" Henderson for this Kiwi group ride with a 10 min blast at the finish. Similar to the Thursday Kiwi Crew Ride. we'll be at 2.0-2.5 w per kg cruising for 35min and jam for the last 10 mins.

#kiwicrew

-----------------------------------------------------------------

Kiwi Crew Riot Race on Monday morning UTC
https://www.zwift.com/events/view/5031758

Kiwi Crew Riot Race
Event Description

This race is a Handicap Chaser / Chop style race with D Cat going first then C-B-A with time gaps in between. The goal is to work as a Team to reach the finish first to claim line honours, can D stay away or will the other categories chase them down before the finish?

Now one of the established major weekly races on the Zwift Calendar every Monday night so get your week off in the best way! the Riot Race sees a regular Crew battling for line honours every week. Pacer Group Auto Cat Join is on and make sure you have a Zwift Power account (www.zwiftpower.com) to be included in the official results!

* Everyone with a Power Meter and / or Smart Trainer is welcome to join.
* New cadence rules in play.
* Winners must have their Strava account linked and are subject to verification checks / requests.

Make sure you check out our other 2 rides, the Kiwi Crew Ride Thursday's at 6:30pm NZ, and the Wakey-Wakey ride, Monday's at 6:00am NZ . Come and join in the fun!

https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftnz

#kiwicrew

name: HerD Beginner Races
description:
Where do you go to try racing or to grow a little better with your racing craft… Herd Beginner Races is set up to provide you with that opportunity by using Zwift Racing Scores (ZRS) with bandwidths that are set up so you’re competing with people of similarities and routes that enable you to challenge yourself and grow with racing. Our pens are set up as;

Pen A   ZRS  345-430

Pen B   ZRS 265-345

Pen C   ZRS 185-265

Pen D   ZRS 115-185

Pen E   ZRS 0-115

If you’re new to racing please take Zwift’s recommendation in blue.

Each week HBR races will have a different focus; racing on a flat route, rolling/undulating with a small climbs, and a route that is commonly used in many Zwift racing events. In addition, the last race of the month will be Tick-Tock so that you can monitor your performance.

name: Bikealicious Climbalicious
description: We are a friendly gathering of cycling enthusiasts. We ride for 120 minutes, and for no less than 20 miles. You can ride with us 30 minutes or the full 2 hours. We ride a new climbing route every Sunday (edt) to keep the workout fresh and fun for all the zwifters. We ride with a rubber band to motivate all skill sets to stay together through challenging climbs resulting in an interactive and dynamic workout. Our group includes riders of all power levels. We welcome all riders.

name: Mountain Massif Group Run
description:
Join Mountain Massif for some relaxed group runs. The goal is to simply complete the route and collect the badge.

More information:
For more information check out the Mountain Massif website www.mountainmassif.com, follow on Instagram, Mountain_Massif and join our Zwift Club, by clicking the link below
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b639575d-930f-4de6-a79d-bea3313350bc/join

name: Know No Bounds Ride
description:
Welcome to the #KnowNoBounds Ride! A ride where we explore the boundaries (or lack thereof) on Zwift.
The ride will begin with 30 minutes at 3 w/kg, utilizing the fence to keep riders together. At the end of the 30 minutes, riders are free to do as they please:
they can stay together and explore the chosen course for the day, or they can head off to the nearest mountain with friends and race it!

When the 30 minute "fenced" portion of the ride is over, we will #KnowNoBounds. Have bike, will travel.
If needed, you will be able to turn to ride in opposite direction on the circuit to reconnect with the group quicker (if you need restroom or are dropped),
and most importantly, the group is not limited to the in-game courses. We can choose to go up a mountain, along a riverside, or any road we want to!

We’re on a mission: to set everyday cyclists free to explore without limits. And we’re going by bike.

Community and camaraderie in cycling is everything: group chat on this ride is highly encouraged!

name: GardaBikeHotel Social Ride
description:
Welcome to Garda Bike Hotel social ride! We are a Bike Hotel on beautiful Lake Garda, Italy. We are specialized in cycling vacations and when here with us, our guests will experience the culture and beauty of our location, the breath taking riding routes, the local cuisine and the wine tasting. We also offer combined tours to other iconic cycling destinations including Stelvio, Dolomites, Piedmont, Adriatic Coast, Rome, Tuscany and Lake Como.

The aim of this inclusive Zwift ride is to have fun, be friendly and connect with our growing community around the world. All of our rides will be led by one of our friendly professional cycling guide. The route will change every week and we will try to keep the ride at around 2.0 watts per kg to make sure everyone can join in and enjoy the fun. We are an official PINARELLO rental centre and on our Zwift rides we will be riding a beautiful Pinarello Dogma F12!

To find out more about Garda Bike Hotel, please visit our website at https://www.gardabikehotel.com/en

Facebook: Garda Bike Hotel
Instagram: gardabikehotel

name: GTR - Alpe Du Zwift Race
description:
Race the full Road to Sky route and finish with a climb up Zwift's most famous road, the Alpe Du Zwift. 

Race in pace categories A-D. 

This race is hosted by the Good Time Riders, GTR, an online bunch of friends from across the world who enjoy riding, racing hard, and generally having a good time together. Everyone is very welcome, you can find us on the Companion App, Facebook and Discord.

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: ZSUN Sunday Social Ride
description:
ZSUN’s flagship Sunday ride since 2016, this long-standing group event offers a friendly, supportive experience with a strong sense of community. Choose the pen that suits your pace and enjoy the shared route experience.

Pen C starts first (2.4–2.6 w/kg), with Pen D (1.5–1.7 w/kg) following shortly after – giving riders the chance to drop back and find their sweet spot.

🗓️
Sundays | 60–120 mins | 2 pens

🚩
Pen C (2.4–2.6 w/kg) | CAT C

🚩
Pen D (1.5–1.7 w/kg) | CAT D

👥
Multiple leaders & sweepers

💬
Friendly chat + Discord optional

🎁
Ride and unlock the ZSUN kit

⚠️
Fence may be used to keep the group together

A great way to end your week with a steady effort and great company.

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - ZG25 Climb Champs
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Rhino Crash Chase Race
description:
🏁 Rhino Crash Chase Race – Team Up, Stay Away, Win Big!
Ready to flip the script on solo racing? In the Rhino Chase, it’s all about teamwork within your category to outpace the others and reach the line first. Set aside rivalries and ride smart—your group’s success depends on it.

🚴 Staggered Category Start:
Category D kicks off first, followed by C, then B, and finally A.
The goal? Stay away and avoid being caught by the chasing categories!

📊 Race Format:
Standard Zwift categories with category enforcement in effect.
Weekly adjustments to time gaps and distances based on course selection to keep finishes tight and thrilling.

⚙️ Requirements:
Power Meter required to enter the pen.
Heart Rate Monitor mandatory for official results.

🎙️ Join the Conversation: Hop into the Rhino Discord (Public Crash voice channel) where we’ll be calling time gaps and cheering each other on. Everyone’s welcome!

https://discord.com/invite/wPD4HJ6

name: HCT Unleashed : Sunday Service
description:
Welcome to HCT Unleashed: Sunday Service.

The week is almost over. Have you paid your dues to the Cycling Gods? This is your final chance to empty the tank and find redemption in a short, sharp Sunday evening sufferfest.

The format is a chaotic mass start, with all categories unleashed at once. But here's the twist: while you can work with—or against—anyone on the course, your official results are only against riders in your own category.

Use the bunch to your advantage. Bridge a gap with a stronger rider or launch a late attack. It’s all part of the game. Join us every week for 20-40 minutes of tactical, high-octane racing.

If you like racing with HCT or are just looking for a racing community to get involved with, join the team on Zwift Power - ""Herts County Triathletes"" https://zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=19017 - and Captain Dan will be in touch to get you set up with the Discord Server. 
On the otherhand if you prefer some less intense involvement HCT also host a Z2 ride @ 3.15pm (GMT) on Saturdays. You can also join the Club on Zwift Companion App, and find us on YouTube, Facebook and Instagram.

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: #EDDK – Super Sunday Race
description:
#EDDK presents #EDDK Super Sunday Recon Race

Nothing but a good solid race.
Race fair, have fun.

RULES:
- All racers using zPower will be DQ
Heart rate monitors compulsory for all categories
You need to have a registered account with Zwiftpower.com and accepted their terms and conditions to be in the results of the race.

Categories:
A: ZRS 690-1000
B: ZRS 520-690
C: ZRS 350-520
D: ZRS 180-350
E: ZRS 0-180

name: Zwift Racing League Recon Ride (Good Time Riders GTR)
description:
This ZRL Group Recon Ride is led by Good Time Riders (GTR) (https://www.zwift.com/clubs/10bd6884-1218-4698-af77-9fda887d4aa7).

Zwift Racing League Group Recon Rides are the official non-competitive Group Rides. Each one is hosted/run by a participating club and will feature racing tips and tricks for the race ahead, an opportunity to ask questions relating to Zwift Racing League and to preview the full course for Tuesday’s ZRL races.

THE AIM
With experienced ride leaders and Zwifters from a reputable Zwift Club, this is your opportunity to ask questions about all things ZRL-related. You'll be able to learn useful course and team tactics and if you are new to ZRL - perhaps even find a team to race with too!

There are multiple Group Recon Rides to choose from each day at various times and each will operate at the advertised paces (pace is based on your Zwift Pace Group).

WHAT IS THE ZRL?
Introducing the spectacular Zwift Racing League (ZRL) - a turbocharged online bike racing extravaganza that's rewriting the rules of competition. With a staggering 180 community divisions, 1,800 electrifying teams, and a whopping 14,000 fierce racers per 6-week round, the ZRL stands as the undisputed heavyweight champion of online bike racing competitions worldwide.

This high-octane spectacle is masterfully orchestrated by our powerhouse partners, WTRL, who leave no stone unturned in delivering an unparalleled racing experience. But remember, this race is an invitation-only affair, so if you dare to join the ranks of speed demons and thrill-seekers, you'll need to hitch a ride with one of the esteemed existing teams. Fear not, though, for WTRL's website (www.WTRL.racing) is your portal to all the insider intel - schedule, rules, and even a team that perfectly aligns with your fiery spirit!

If you want to gather your own team, you register for the next Round of ZRL at www.wtrl.racing/zwift-racing-league/.

#zrlrecon #wtrl #zrl #dirt

eventSubgroups.name: Zwift Racing League Recon Ride (Good Time Riders GTR) (E)

name: TFC Group Group Ride Sub 2
description:
The TFC ride is open to all riders on Zwift, if you have not already, please opt in to Zwiftpower or you will not be included in the Zwiftpower results screen.
Please follow the yellow beacon who will be the designated ride lead and obey any instructions. Please ensure no TT bikes to benefit from the group draft.
C Group

The sweep team will try to pick up any dropped riders and bring them back to the group if they can hold 2.2-2.3wkg.
During the last lap the ride leader will call for a race to the flag. Wait for the ride leaders’ instructions before you go.

D Group

The D Group will be a Sub 2 W/Kg. The sweep team will pick up dropped riders and bring them back into the main pack. Remember this is the ride average so there will be times the group is slightly above the advertised W/Kg and times when it is below. Listen to the ride lead to ensure everybody is in the main group.

BEWARE - If you do drop off the back of the group you will need to ride at a considerable higher pace to get back to the group. The Sweep teams are there to tow you back on if you do drop off,
We will be on Discord: https://discord.gg/yU6Gkcu
If you’re interested in joining Team TFC please check out: www.facebook.com/groups/teamtfc

name: RollCo: Night Shift
description:
Night Shift is Rolling Collectives after-hours ritual race.

When the lights drop and the noise fades, this is where racing gets raw. Short, sharp courses. Mixed terrain.  No hiding behind long climbs or safe pacing. From the first pedal stroke, it’s on.
Night Shift is built for riders who race on feel and instinct. Attacks come hard, selections happen fast, and hesitation costs you. You don’t wait for the race to start, you make it.

This is late-night racing for those who like it intense, imperfect and alive. All in, after dark.

Why Join Rolling Collective?
We’re more than a club — we’re a global community of riders who support each other, grow together, and celebrate every ride, whether it’s a grind, a badge hunt, or a race. Join us on Discord, connect with fellow riders, and be part of something bigger.

Forward, Together

name: The Grind Finale
description:
ABOUT THIS WORKOUT

This session aims to replicate the demands of a mountain bike ride / event. 

Mountain biking generally involves far more variations in effort compared with road riding - so plenty of surges and time spent being over geared! We also spend a lot of time on and off the power, so short spells going  hard to get over an obstacle, followed by a freewheel or very low power on a descent or little dip.

We touch upon all of these aspects in todays session, as we start off alternating every 30 seconds between riding hard at a low cadence, and riding easy - as if we are riding through an undulating single track. Then we make a ramp in effort, which finishes with a 30 second near max effort up a steep, rocky banking!

WORKOUT DESIGNER

Matt Rowe - Heavily involved in cycling for the past 25 years, Matt represented Great Britain at the Junior World Championships before graduating to the pro ranks. Matt draws on his extensive race experience to help amateur cyclists around the world achieve their personal goals. 

ABOUT GROUP WORKOUTS

Zwifters line up in the starting area just like a normal event and once the event starts, workout mode begins. Everyone stays together regardless of power output. That's right, if you're outputting 100W and another Zwifter is outputting 400W you will stay in exactly the same position relative to each other. Only if you stop pedaling and fall 6 meters behind the group will the rubber-banding stop. 

We highly recommend doing one of the FTP tests as the estimated FTP can be inaccurate and make the difficulty level of the workouts too high for your current fitness level. For more info on FTP go to: https://support.zwift.com/hc/en-us/articles/210208083-What-is-FTP-and-how-is-it-used-in-Zwift

eventSubgroups.name: The Grind Finale (E)
eventSubgroups.description: #zwiftofficial

name: EVO CC Sprint Race Series
description:
CATEGORY ENFORCEMENT IS ENABLED FOR ALL EVO RACES!

What is Category Enforcement? 

Category Enforcement will prevent Zwifters from entering a category they are not eligible for.  Our goal is to increase fairness in racing.

HOW DOES IT WORK?

- Zwift checks a racer's past performance using VO2 max and FTP to determine their race category.
- Racers can join a category at or above their category. For example, if you are "C" category, you can race in C, B, or A category. You will not be able to race in the D category and the option to join will be absent.

Welcome to EVO sprintrace series. Shorter more compact races for the rider looking for an effective high intensity race. Start fast, hang on in the middle, then sprint for the win at the finish. Expect to push your heart rate to its maximum. Sprinkle a few of these into your training plan and watch your fitness grow.

To make racing as fair as possible we have enforced a few minor rules that will result in automatic disqualification on ZwiftPower.

All riders must wear a heart rate monitor
Riding in an incorrect category.

zPower riders will be disqualified in A, B, and C Podium. zPower is allowed in the D category. Race details and final results will be available at www.zwiftpower.com

#evo

name: VTO Monday Recovery Ride
description:
With a focus of FUN 😂, FRIENDSHIP 👫 and DETERMINATION 💪🏻, TEAM VTO is where the Australia & NZ Triathlon Community get together to chat.

We welcome all Multisport & Paratriathletes from around the world 🌍 for a 1 hour social ride. 🏊🏼‍♂️🚴🏼‍♂️🏃🏻‍♂️🇦🇺. It's Monday Morning 🥱 so the pace will be a gentle 2-2.5 w/kg (but we will vary for the group).

Please stay together as a group around the Beacon and feel free to get involved in the chat💬. It is a group ride - NOT a race!

Join our Zwift Club in your Companion app 📲 https://www.zwift.com/clubs/151ca95c-f6de-4fe2-ae88-a769e367f439/home

All are welcome to be involved in our community, join our FB group;
www.facebook.com/groups/thenewzwifttrioz/

Don't get #FOMO 😨 - join us today 👍🏻😊🤒

name: Stage 1 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 1
Route: Flat Out Fast
Date: March 16-22

The adventure kicks off on Flat Out Fast—where the name of the game is no hills and Big Spin prizes galore!

name: Sunday Funday p/b TBR
description:
Come hang with some friendly peeps on TBR’s D-friendly ride! We roll together in a big, happy bunch at an average 2.0w/kg pace.

We don’t use the fence in favor of control and respect for the ride leader and listed pace. Our rides are fence free and whinge free, if you stick to the leader’s Yellow Beacon like a bear to honey you will enjoy the benefits of a group draft along with lots of friendly banter. We want you to have a happy spin on the bike!

We may be The Big Ring and known for powerful riders, but our tent is also big and we encourage riders comfortable at a social pace to meet our friendly crew and learn about the TBR Effect. We are all about supporting riders in a friendly environment and helping people feel comfortable gradually working up to a faster pace. At TBR, we love to help riders look inside themselves and discover the hidden power within.

Follow The Big Ring (TBR) at www.thebigring.cc

description:
Come and roll with AHDR in a social bunch at around 2.5-2.8W/kg.

Please stay together as a group around our Beacon for a steady endurance ride.

Fun, Teamwork and Respect for each other will always be the focus!

All Welcome inc. 30min Rejoin/Late Join available.

Roll with us for as long as you want.

^Smiles Mandatory *Snacks Optional.

#ahdr

name: The HERD Sunday Sip and Spin
description:
Hello ladies of Zwift! Come join the Herd ladies as we explore all the flats, hills, and everything in between on this banded ladies only ride.
As long as you keep spinning, at whatever pace you want, we will stay together. Want to tootle along at .5 w/kg? It’s all good. Want to get a high-powered workout in at 3 w/kg? Awesome! You’ll pull us along! Want to do intervals? That works too!
The only time we won’t do a banded ride is when we’re having a sprint practice ride. Otherwise, together we shall stay!
So, grab your favorite drink to Sip and come join us as we Spin further, climb higher and get stronger together!

Don't forget to join us on Discord. Download the app from https://discord.gg/Dr7ZtPV and use the “Ride Audio” channel.
Check out our website: theherd.club for a link to all our rides, Facebook, Strava and our sister racing league.

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: LGBTQz Ride with Pride
description:
Everyone is welcome to join LGBTQ Zwifters and friends as we Ride with Pride all
year round in our Sunday social ride!
Our yellow beacons aim to maintain an average social pace of around 1.6 - 1.8
W/Kg, and we encourage everyone to ride close to yellow and enjoy being part of
our rainbow blob. Please be aware that we frequently use the rubberband in our group rides. You can check the event details to see if this is the case for this ride by using the link 'Toggle Event Details' at the end of the event description on zwift.com. Ride leaders may have quizzes or other fun
stuff planned for you, so please do get involved using the companion app chat
function. Our rides are inclusive, we value diversity and courage, and we are always
respectful. Ride chat may involve LGBTQIA+ topics so please come with an open
heart and open mind.
If you want to learn more about what to expect on one of our rides, or find links to our
Club and socials, please visit: https://lgbtq-zwifters.com/

name: WMZ Bonjour Recovery & Optional Sprints (p/b Champion Systems)
description:
Waratah Masters is a cycling club based in Sydney Australia. This is a recovery ride from any long weekend rides and racing, this ride includes an optional sprint segment each lap (approx every 7 minutes). It is expected everyone will stay together during the ride: no flyers. 

The ride and Leader (Yellow Beacon) averages w/kg is 1.9. 

If sprinting, please fall back to the yellow beacon after the sprint.  Bigger group = more inclusive and faster sprint times.

As well as the Zwift sprint time leaderboard the ride leader will announce the most powerful riders, so come test yourself just for fun.

The ride is a chance for weekend recovery, a friendly chat and a chance to see how much power you can put down if sprinting, or just watch others dropping watt bombs while you take it easy.

Note: on rare occasions this event may be cancelled for a given week, usually due to Australian public holidays (leader availability).

Stage 2
Route: Mayan San Remo
Date: March 16-23

name: JETT Monday Morning Ride (2.5~3.0w/kg)
description:
Welcome to Japan eSports Tempting Team Monday Morning Ride !!
Power : ave 2.5w/kg〜3.0w/kg
Of course, ZWIFTer of all countries can participate. Leaders and heads pull groups at 2.5~3.0w/kg. Riders who are hard at the above power should try grouping by using drafting well.
If you leave the group behind, please offer with ""HELP !!"" and ZWIFT CAHT. Sweeper goes for support. Sweeper will not go for support unless he offers ""HELP !!"". Also, please offer within 10 seconds of time difference as much as possible.

Rule of this group ride
· You always keep grouping in mind( this is not a race )
· Do not Lead from Leader - yellow beacon
· Power Ups allowed ( do not use final Sprint )
· TT Bike allowed
Follow the leader's instructions, let's work together and make the best group, the best workout!

JETT Monday Morning Ride
パワー ave.2.5w/kg〜3.0w/kg
Japan Monday Morning Ride! もちろん、全ての国のZWIFTerが参加できます。 リーダーおよび先頭は2.5〜3.0w/kgでグループを引っ張ります。上記のパワーでは辛いというライダーはドラフティングをうまく活用してグルーピングにトライして下さい。
もし、集団から後方に離れてしまった場合、「HELP!!」とZWIFT CAHTで申し出てください。Sweeperが支援に向かいます。「HELP!!」の申し出がない限り、Sweeperは支援には行きません。また、可能な限りタイム差１０秒以内に申し出をお願いします。
このグループライドのルール
・常にグルーピングを心がけること(レースではありません）
・リーダーの黄色いビーコンよりも前に出ないこと（２０m以上）
・TT Bike OK
・スプリントはリーダー指示に従いましょう
50分のGroup Ride、リーダー指示に従い、みんなで協力して最高のグループ、最高のワークアウトにしましょう！
#JETT

Follow the leader's instructions, let's work together and make the best group, the best workout!

JETT Monday Morning Ride
パワー ave.2.5w/kg〜3.0w/kg
Japan Monday Morning Ride! もちろん、全ての国のZWIFTerが参加できます。 リーダーおよび先頭は2.5〜3.0w/kgでグループを引っ張ります。上記のパワーでは辛いというライダーはドラフティングをうまく活用してグルーピングにトライして下さい。
もし、集団から後方に離れてしまった場合、「HELP!!」とZWIFT CAHTで申し出てください。Sweeperが支援に向かいます。「HELP!!」の申し出がない限り、Sweeperは支援には行きません。また、可能な限りタイム差１０秒以内に申し出をお願いします。
このグループライドのルール
・常にグルーピングを心がけること(レースではありません）
・リーダーの黄色いビーコンよりも前に出ないこと（２０m以上）
・TT Bike OK
・スプリントはリーダー指示に従いましょう
50分のGroup Ride、リーダー指示に従い、みんなで協力して最高のグループ、最高のワークアウトにしましょう！
#JETT

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Downtown Titans
Date: March 23-30


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Downtown Titans—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: TTR Cat Claw Crawl
description: Welcome to the "Monday Morning Cat Claw Crawl" Ride. This ride is based on 2.8 w/kg, with the pace increasing as needed. It may include light workouts. We go faster and more comfortably with the power of the bunch. For "Be stronger"

eventSubgroups.name: TTR Cat Claw Crawl (C)

name: Ascenders Team Asia Morning Group Run
description:
Come and join the Ascenders Team as we put on our running shoes and run
daily with our friends too by starting our day in a healthy way. Research
shows that just 30 minutes of running can have huge benefits on your
short-term and long-term health. So we designed our running events to
motivate athletes to run 30 minutes each day to boost your metabolism and
use those calories. Running will also help you look good and feel stronger.

Our group run is designed with runners of all levels in mind and set the
pace range to be determined by our Zwifter Runners. So find a group pace
that matches how you feel each day and make those steps count for you.
Runners who went beyond the event duration will find that we have selected
courses that will help you earn the Route Badges when you run the full
distance.

So lace up those running shoes as everybody is welcome to join with our
friendly and motivating runners.

# Join Ascenders Team on Facebook:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/AscendersZwift
# Ascenders Discord Channel: https://discord.gg/cBfQ5jkrPh

eventSubgroups.name: Ascenders Team Asia Morning Group Run

name: Zwift Racing League Recon Ride (Velo Optimum)
description:
This ZRL Group Recon Ride is led by Vélo Optimum (https://www.zwift.com/clubs/9106af3d-c21d-4b40-9930-aaca5392b7f5).

Zwift Racing League Group Recon Rides are the official non-competitive Group Rides. Each one is hosted/run by a participating club and will feature racing tips and tricks for the race ahead, an opportunity to ask questions relating to Zwift Racing League and to preview the full course for Tuesday’s ZRL races.

THE AIM
With experienced ride leaders and Zwifters from a reputable Zwift Club, this is your opportunity to ask questions about all things ZRL-related. You'll be able to learn useful course and team tactics and if you are new to ZRL - perhaps even find a team to race with too!

There are multiple Group Recon Rides to choose from each day at various times and each will operate at the advertised paces (pace is based on your Zwift Pace Group).

WHAT IS THE ZRL?
Introducing the spectacular Zwift Racing League (ZRL) - a turbocharged online bike racing extravaganza that's rewriting the rules of competition. With a staggering 180 community divisions, 1,800 electrifying teams, and a whopping 14,000 fierce racers per 6-week round, the ZRL stands as the undisputed heavyweight champion of online bike racing competitions worldwide.

This high-octane spectacle is masterfully orchestrated by our powerhouse partners, WTRL, who leave no stone unturned in delivering an unparalleled racing experience. But remember, this race is an invitation-only affair, so if you dare to join the ranks of speed demons and thrill-seekers, you'll need to hitch a ride with one of the esteemed existing teams. Fear not, though, for WTRL's website (www.WTRL.racing) is your portal to all the insider intel - schedule, rules, and even a team that perfectly aligns with your fiery spirit!

If you want to gather your own team, you register for the next Round of ZRL at www.wtrl.racing/zwift-racing-league/.

#zrlrecon #wtrl #zrl #dirt

name: EZR Weekend Wind Down
description:
All riders are welcome to join the EZ Riders (EZR) for this relaxed pace social group ride. Whether you are new to Zwift or group rides, looking for a recovery/rehab ride, or just looking to ride with a supportive group of friends, this ride is for you. The ride leader will be staying within the advertised average pace of this ride as we explore Zwift’s various worlds. Please respect the ride leader’s pace and ride with the yellow beacon.

Join us on Discord during the ride: https://discord.gg/kmfS4YN 

Facebook Page: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZER.Cycling

#zer #ezr #ezriders

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Volcano Circuit CCW
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: GTR - Alpe Du Zwift Race
description:
Race the full Road to Sky route and finish with a climb up Zwift's most famous road, the Alpe Du Zwift. 

Race in pace categories A-D. 

This race is hosted by the Good Time Riders, GTR, an online bunch of friends from across the world who enjoy riding, racing hard, and generally having a good time together. Everyone is very welcome, you can find us on the Companion App, Facebook and Discord.

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: Stage 1 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 1
Route: Flat Out Fast
Date: March 16-22

The adventure kicks off on Flat Out Fast—where the name of the game is no hills and Big Spin prizes galore!

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: ZLDR AYOP Event
description:
Start your day on the right foot or the left. Come to our 5k at your own pace (AYOP). Run, walk, chat, laugh and get your day moving on a positive note! Attitude is 90% of any situation so let’s make this day the best day and get you in the best mindset so you can be the best you!

ZLDR is a group of diverse people, from speed demons to walkers, from people just getting to their first 5k to ultra runners. We are here to support your running dreams and help you develop your running skills. We are a collective group of supportive and experienced runners and walkers who want to help you set and achieve new goals!

For more ZLDR fun and support, join our groups on social media platforms:

Facebook Group: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ZLDR2020
Strava Club: https://www.strava.com/clubs/zldr2020
Instagram page: https://www.instagram.com/zldr.2020/
Discord Invitation (not required for the run)
https://discord.com/invite/PuFctEnWyn
ZLDR website for Challenge Months: https://www.zldr.club
Online shop: https://www.sweatydino.com

name: OTR Tempo Group Ride
description:
Come join OTR for our weekly Tempo Group Ride. Courses will rotate weekly on shorter circuit routes so slower riders off the back have a chance to rejoin the group. The Yellow Beacon ride leaders will guide you through a mix of sprints, tempo, and recovery laps. Be careful, the fence is live and will zap you if you fly off the front! The final lap is an optional race pace lap and a chance to give it all you got!

B group will focus on riding together at an average pace between 2.8 - 3.2 w/kg.
D group will focus on riding together at an average pace between 1.8 - 2.2 w/kg.

Join us for some conversation during the ride on our OTR discord channel at: https://discord.gg/WjY7EuAMv4

If you are interested in racing with OTR please reach out to any OTR team member. We are happy to help you meet your racing goals!

name: Bikealicious Altitude with Attitude
description:
We are a friendly gathering of cycling enthusiasts. We ride for 60 minutes and throw some badge hunt here and there. Bikealicious assigns new routes every Sunday (EST)/Monday (UTC) to keep the workout fresh and fun for all the shifters. We ride with a rubber band to motivate all skill sets to stay together through challenging routes, resulting in an interactive and dynamic workout. Our group includes riders of all power levels. We welcome all riders. 

Ride On!

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Volcano Circuit CCW
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

eventSubgroups.name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro (A)
NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro (B)

name: Mountain Massif-Vekta Adaptative Training
description:
This is session is a taster of what is available if you subscribe to Vekta’s 16 Gran Fondo plan, brought
to you by Cigala Cycling.
Created by Matteo Cigala, who has multiple Gran Fondo victories to his name, this structured
training session is a sample from Vekta’s Gran Fondo Adaptive Plan. For more information about the
plan visit:
Gran Fondo Adaptive Plan | Vekta
Safety Note:
Only undertake vigorous exercise if you are fit and healthy to do so. If you are at all unsure, consult a
Physician or Doctor first. All training equipment including Bikes and Turbo trainers should be in full
working order. Do not use any piece of equipment that does not come up to the required standard.
More information:
For more information check out the Mountain Massif website www.mountainmassif.com, follow on
Facebook, Instagram (Mountain_Massif) and join our Zwift Club by clicking the following link
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/mountainmassif/join.
To record all your training data and chart your progress, consider using the Vekta training app, which
you can read about here: https://www.mountainmassif.com/reviews/software/vekta-a-first-glance-
at-the-new-training-platform/

name: Cycle Nation Monday Endurance Masterblaster
description:
Welcome to the Cycle Nation Monday Endurance Masterblaster. Join us for an metric century endurance ride to start your week the right way.
The ride is structured in three parts:
The first tempo section is a steady, controlled effort at 2.8-3.0w/kg to settle in.
Then comes the twist - approximately 10 minutes at 3.0-3.5w/kg.
We finish with a steady block at 2.8-3.0w/kg, with optional sprints, focusing on control, consistency and finishing together.
All riders are welcome to join the event and enjoy the friendly atmosphere. Please follow the ride leaders instructions and group around the beacon. Most importantly, this isn’t a race and we ask that you respect the pace set by the ride leader which will allow for an enjoyable group ride experience for all.
Connect with Cycle Nation:
Visit our website: www.cyclenation.co.za
Join our Facebook Group: https://bit.ly/38TTtAh
Join our Zwift Team: https://bit.ly/3D2Q4zL
Join our Strava Club: https://bit.ly/3pEDkFM
Instagram: https://bit.ly/3pEsdN6

Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!


Stage 4
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 6

Wrap up the Zwift Big Spin down on Southern Coast Cruise, with loads of prizes still on deck!

name: The Zombie 5k
description:
Brought to you from the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) this event is called the Zombie Run as for those in the UK this event is in the middle of the night.

Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join, you can run at whatever pace you like, it's not a race.
If you don't want to run 5k then come along for as long as you wish, the support is always appreciated.
The event runs at the same time each day, Tuesday to Saturday and each day there is a different course.

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/

https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Follow Stuart on Strava - https://www.strava.com/athletes/2480515

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: Vikings Valhalla Monday Magic
description:
Come and start your week with the Vikings Valhalla Monday Magic.

This is a great ride for new Zwifters, people looking for some fat burning or people looking for a recovery ride.

We will divide the 45 minutes into 3 15 minute blocks. We start out at an easy pace, and increase the pace a little in each block.

All blocks and our average watts will be in the 1.8-2.2 w/kg area.

name: Team Italy Morning Early Ride
description:
WELCOME TO ALL RIDERS!
Team Italy Morning Early Ride is an easy group ride SUB 2.0 w/kg and NOT a race.
Follow the leader (yellow beacon) instructions during the ride.
The sweeper (red beacon) will support the leader and give help to the riders in the rear of the group.
Your jersey will be autoselected on Team Italy kit and unlock it to the end of the ride.
Please, add tag [ITA] after your name.
This is the Facebook group of Team Italy https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftitaly and Strava club of Team Italy https://www.strava.com/clubs/zwift_italy
Join us! Have fun! Ride On!.

name: GXY BOOST! INTERVAL GROUP RIDE [3.0-4.0 wkg] – CAT B
description:
Welcome to GXY BOOST!
This is a twice weekly interval event which is targeted at riders looking to develop strength and endurance.
The events are open to all riders not just category B , but also those moving from rides of lower intensity.
BOOST! rides will be generally focusing between Vo2 and Threshold so expect the average wkg to finish between the
advertised power range based on a 75kg rider .
The sessions will be between 60 -90 minutes duration depending on intensity, course chosen or ride leader.
There will be no red sweeping beacon, so if you drop from the group simply continue the workout at your own pace.
We will of course continue to support you throughout the ride, just hang on in there and try and make it through to the
end!
Feel free to join in the fun during the ride by using the messaging system or if you prefer our dedicated BOOST!
Discord channel.
GXY are a friendly and supportive club who strive to help others succeed and we are of course looking forward to
riding with you.
If you want to become a member and join our community to race, ride socially, or both, please come and join ideally
via our Facebook link.
https://www.facebook.com/groups/galaxycyclingclub/
https://www.strava.com/clubs/989894
https://www.instagram.com/galaxycyclingclub

eventSubgroups.name: GXY BOOST! INTERVAL GROUP RIDE [3.0-4.0 wkg] – CAT B (B)

name: EVO CC Vortex Interval Ride [~3-3.8w/kg avg]
description:
Welcome to the Evolution Cycling Club [EVO] interval session. These rides are geared towards Zwifters that are looking for a varied content one hour session that can give them a full 5 zone workout. Draft in the group and stay zone 1-3, or move to the front and join the sprints for zones 4 and 5 for short bursts. Average for this ride will be mid to high Category B but it may vary based upon course/leader. There will be keepers at the back, and they will try hard to save dropped riders. The group is based upon high social interaction so bring your best banter. Use the mobile app or a keyboard to communicate in-ride.

#evo

name: EVO CC Flux Ride [1.8 - 2.0w/kg avg]
description:
This is a Evolution Cycling Club [EVO] Social Event and ride for all. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that all riders respect the pace set by the leader (Yellow Beacon). We may include intervals over and below the ride average advertised power range, but we will allow enough recovery and time to regroup after every effort. This is not a race, so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong we would welcome any help offered by stronger riders to bring any dropped riders back to the yellow beacon. We welcome good chat and a social atmosphere for new and experienced Zwifters alike, everyone is welcome.

#evo

name: SZR Early Birdies
description:
Welcome to SZR Early birdies

This is a group ride with the focus on remaining together. You should aim to sit between the yellow and red beacon. Follow ride leader instructions at all times.

We will ride at an average pace of 2.5 w/kg prior to any race finish. The race finish is at the discretion of the ride leader and will vary in length dependent on the ride.

If there are any sprints allowed during the ride please regroup on the yellow beacon immediately after.

After hills we will focus on getting the back of the group together with the front.

#SZR

name: 3R SPARK Steady State Ride [~2.0-2.2 w/kg avg]
description: This is a 3R Group Ride Event (not a race). 3R Spark runs five days a week and is designed to be an accessible and sociable training session, aimed primarily at riders in Cat-D. Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 2.0-2.5 W/kg. The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides. Mondays and Tuesdays are our entry-level steady-state rides, where we aim to hold a steady 2.0-2.2 throughout. Thursdays and Fridays are our interval sessions, designed to help Cat D riders push themselves a little further. For interval sessions we aim to average between 2.2-2.5 for the session, but there will be times above and below. Thursday and Friday sessions often include an optional mini-race at the end. Wednesday is the Taylor Zwift Fan Club ride, a led by the hosts of the popular podcast of the same name – expect a sociable steady-state session with lots to keep you entertained! The TZFC ride is livestreamed at twitch.tv/TaylorZwiftFC. Rides are typically around an hour long. Spark is a great introduction to group rides on Zwift, we are a friendly and supportive group who love to see one another succeed. We always have a sweeper (red beacon) whose wheel you can grab if you get dropped. There’s always plenty of good chat, via in-ride messages or on Discord. Come and join us! Join the 3R Facebook group for more info: https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #3R #RideRaceRaise

name: 3R Octane Interval Ride [~3.2-3.6 w/kg avg]
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride Event. 3R Octane runs five days a week and is designed to provide a solid and fun training platform for riders.

Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 3.2-3.8 W/kg.

The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides, including weekly recovery, or transitional, rides which are usually on a Monday. Short, high intensity intervals, longer sweetspot intervals, flying laps, long and short climbs and exploring little used routes all feature.

The rides are typically around an hour long, but some can be up to two hours long, and all end with a mini race.

The Group welcomes rider interaction through messaging and the Octane discord server and features a super bunch of regulars to which newcomers, and riders stepping up from lower intensity rides, are always welcome. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/

#Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise #3r

name: 3R VOLT Interval Ride [~2.6-3.2 w/kg avg]
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack. 3R VOLT runs five days a week and is designed to provide a solid and fun training platform for riders.

Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 2.8-3.2 w/kg.

The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides and training needs, the following is a typical week:
Monday – After what has hopefully been a fun backed weekend on the bike, this our recovery session
Tuesday – Tends to be more of a classic interval session, riders usually finish with an average of around 3.0w/kg
Wednesday – We take to the hills and one of our tougher sessions with riders averaging around 3.2w/kg
Thursday – is our no red beacon ride and again tough interval sets and riders finishing around 3.2w/kg
Friday - is a little different, we don't take ourselves too seriously and often play a variety of games and quizzes along side the intervals and riders end up with around 2.8-3.0w/kg

The rides are typically around an hour long, but some may take up to 75mins with the majority finishing with either a ramp or a mini race.

The group welcomes rider interaction through messaging and the VOLT discord server and features a friendly bunch of regulars to which newcomers, and riders stepping up from lower intensity rides, are always welcome. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/

This group ride is not a race so. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise.

eventSubgroups.name: 3R VOLT Interval Ride [~2.6-3.2 w/kg avg] (C)
eventSubgroups.description:
This is a 3R Group Ride so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack. 3R VOLT runs five days a week and is designed to provide a solid and fun training platform for riders.

Different ride leaders target different effort levels and courses, with power averages in the range 2.8-3.2 w/kg.

The wide spread reflects the diversity of rides and training needs, the following is a typical week:
Monday – After what has hopefully been a fun backed weekend on the bike, this our recovery session
Tuesday – Tends to be more of a classic interval session, riders usually finish with an average of around 3.0w/kg
Wednesday – We take to the hills and one of our tougher sessions with riders averaging around 3.2w/kg
Thursday – is our no red beacon ride and again tough interval sets and riders finishing around 3.2w/kg
Friday - is a little different, we don't take ourselves too seriously and often play a variety of games and quizzes along side the intervals and riders end up with around 2.8-3.0w/kg

The rides are typically around an hour long, but some may take up to 75mins with the majority finishing with either a ramp or a mini race.

The group welcomes rider interaction through messaging and the VOLT discord server and features a friendly bunch of regulars to which newcomers, and riders stepping up from lower intensity rides, are always welcome. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/

This group ride is not a race so. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise.

name: Workout 2 - Zwift Camp: Breakthrough
description:
Outdoor miles are right around the corner, and these five Zwift Camp: Breakthrough workouts get you ready for them. Sharpen your speed, smooth your pedal stroke, and build the strength you need for hills and longer rides outside.

Workout 2: Body Control
This one’s got a little bit of everything: single-leg drills, hand positioning, and calm upper-body control are all on deck, keeping you confident and smooth from start to finish.

name: GXY SURGE [1.6-2.5wkg] – CAT D
description:
Welcome to GXY Surge!
 
Different ride leaders will target varying effort levels each day, yet still bring you home well within the advertised power!
 
The ride is looking to push Cat Ds and low Cat Cs that little further all within a manageable 50 minute ride.  Please respect the leader’s pace and watch your fitness develop!
 
GXY are a friendly and supportive club who strive to help others succeed.  We are looking forward to riding with you.
 
If you want to become a member and join our community to race, ride socially, or both, please come and join ideally via our Facebook link or by the email below:
 
https://www.facebook.com/groups/galaxycyclingclub/
https://www.strava.com/clubs/989894
https://www.instagram.com/galaxycyclingclub

name: Stage 2: Pas Racing: Handful of Gravel
description:
The PAS Racing Gravel Series brings the spirit and style of Pas Normal Studios to gravel racing on Zwift. Across four stages, you’ll take on demanding routes in a series designed to be raced flat out. Get a shiny new gravel bike from the Drop Shop and let it rip!

Complete any stage to win a brand new Pas Normal Jersey!

Badges are out—XP is in. Every finish fuels your progress. Earn up to 2000 XP for completing the series, including a massive 1000 XP from Stage One.

Stage 1
Route: Jungle Circuit
Date: April 6–12

The series kicks off on the Jungle Circuit in Watopia.. Line up, find your rhythm, and open the PAS Racing Series strong.

Stage 2
Route: Handful of Gravel
Date: April 13–19

The challenge builds. Handful of Gravel rewards the right setup and steady power across demanding terrain. Upgrade your build, join the next start line, and keep the momentum rolling.

Stage 3
Route: Yumezi Grit
Date: April 20–26

A brand new gravel racing route on Zwift. Fresh terrain. New lines to chase. Take on Yumezi Grit, test yourself against the field, and experience competitive gravel at its best.

Stage 4
Route: Serpentine 8
Date: April 27 – May 3

One final chance to line up and close out the PAS Racing Series. Hard routes. Real racing. Finish it strong.

MONTHLY GC COMPETITION

Looking for extra motivation? The Monthly GC (General Classification) Competition lets you track your progress across all stages. Each race counts—your times add up throughout the series, shaping the overall standings. At the end of the month, the fastest total time takes the win!
VIEW RESULTS: https://www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/pasracing2026

—------------------

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that in order to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

name: SZR Sunrise Ride
description:
Welcome to SZR Sunrise

This is a group ride with the focus on remaining together.
The average pace PRIOR to any race finish will be 2.2 wkg. Suitable for D riders looking to become C’s and above
Sprints and intervals will be added at the discretion of the leader.
We will at times ride above and below the advertised pace, please don’t feel the need to tell the leader they will be aware!

Please regroup quickly after sprints, intervals or hills and sit between the yellow and red beacons.
This is not a recovery ride, we work hard and work as a team.

eventSubgroups.name: SZR Sunrise Ride (E)
eventSubgroups.description:
The focus is on the Group and keeping together while having fun to make the time fly fast!
The ride tempo is shown next to the ride category, this is rider weight / gender dependant.
Note that the average is for the ride prior to any optional faster finish (with a beacon).
The ride leader will aim (prior to any faster finish) to ride mid parameters after a short warmup period.
There might be slight variations above or below the average during the ride.

name: Bikealicious Rubberband Joy Ride
description:
This is a social ride welcoming all riders. Our mission is to push you further than before, build your endurance, and have fun. If you are feeling strong, push to the front and drag the group, if not, just sit in the peloton.

We have the “rubber-band” activated, which means that it’s a no drop ride - no matter what level you are, you will be able to keep up, and the group WILL STAY TOGETHER. Note: The overall speed may be lower than in other rides due to the banding effect.

So hop on that bike, grab those bottles, and let’s ride!

name: Monday Roll Out
description:
The QueenBees are a group of women who share a love of cycling. Just as a hive works together to bring out the best in every member of the community, so do we. We strive to support, empower and encourage women to live their best lives, no matter where they are on their life, cycling or fitness journey. Join us for a social ride, all abilities welcome pace will be around 1.5-1.8 wkg. During the ride try to stay with the yellow lead beacon and follow instructions from your ride leader.

Join our QueenBee hive here (ladies only): https://www.facebook.com/groups/218570110355444
